title: 10 best herbal capsules for indigestion
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for indigestion are dietary supplements that contain a mixture of herbs and plant extracts, designed to alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach discomfort. These natural remedies offer a safe and effective alternative to over-the-counter medications. Examples of herbs commonly used in these capsules include ginger, peppermint, fennel, and artichoke. Ginger, for instance, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive tract, while peppermint oil can relax the gastrointestinal muscles, thereby reducing spasms and promoting the flow of bile. By improving digestion, herbal capsules can enhance nutrient absorption, boost energy levels, and promote overall well-being. Users report reduced discomfort after meals, improved bowel movements, and increased productivity, as they no longer have to worry about indigestion disrupting their daily activities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood capsule helps with indigestion because it contains a powerful compound called artemisinin, which has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes in the stomach. This, in turn, helps to break down food more efficiently, reducing the likelihood of indigestion and other digestive discomforts. Additionally, wormwood has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms of indigestion. Furthermore, wormwood has been shown to improve gut motility, helping to move food through the digestive system more smoothly and preventing the buildup of gas and bloating that can contribute to indigestion. Overall, wormwood capsules offer a natural and effective solution for those struggling with indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood contains compounds such as thujone and isothujone that can help kill off harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, such as H. pylori, which can contribute to indigestion.
Carminative properties: Wormwood has the ability to reduce bloating, gas, and other symptoms of indigestion due to its volatile oils, including alpha- and beta-thujone.
Choleretic properties: Wormwood can increase the production and flow of bile from the liver, which can help improve digestion of fats and reduce symptoms of indigestion.

Spasmolytic properties: Wormwood contains compounds such as flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones that can help relax smooth muscle in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping associated with indigestion.

Bitter properties: Wormwood contains bitter compounds such as absinthin and anabsinthin that can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and improve overall digestion, reducing symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried wormwood herb and size "00" empty capsules, or gather fresh wormwood and allow it to dry completely.
Grind dried wormwood into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, resulting in a consistent texture.
Fill empty capsules with wormwood powder using a capsule filling machine or by hand, allotting 500-600mg of powder per capsule.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dark place in an airtight container to maintain potency.
Consult with a healthcare professional to determine the appropriate dosage and potential interactions with other medications or health conditions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsule helps with indigestion because it contains menthol and methyl salicylate, compounds that have antispasmodic properties, which means they can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles. This action can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and abdominal pain. Additionally, peppermint has been shown to increase bile secretion and speed up the emptying of the stomach, which can further aid in digestion. Overall, peppermint capsules can provide a natural and effective way to manage indigestion and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic: Peppermint capsules can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and pain, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate symptoms such as cramps and bloating. This property is attributed to the menthol and menthone present in peppermint.

Carminative: Peppermint capsules aid in the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, which is crucial for indigestion as it can help relieve symptoms such as bloating and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are menthol and methyl salicylate.

Choleretic: Peppermint capsules can increase bile secretion, which is important for indigestion as it can help improve the digestion and absorption of fats and fat-soluble vitamins. The active constituent responsible for this property is menthol.

Analgesic: Peppermint capsules can help reduce pain, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate symptoms such as abdominal pain and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are menthol and methyl salicylate.

Anxiolytic: Peppermint capsules can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate symptoms such as stress-induced indigestion. The active constituent responsible for this property is menthol.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried peppermint leaves and a capsule-filling machine.
Grind the peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Load the empty capsules into the filling machine, then fill each capsule with the peppermint powder.
Press the two halves of each capsule together to seal and store in an airtight container.
Take 1-2 capsules after meals or as needed for indigestion, following the recommended dosage for adults.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsule helps with indigestion because it contains potent oils, such as fenchone, estragole, and anethole, which have antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal tract muscles, reducing symptoms of indigestion like bloating, gas, and cramps. Fennel's carminative effect helps break down and expel excess gas from the digestive tract, providing quick relief. Additionally, fennel has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, enhancing the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, ultimately promoting healthy digestion. Overall, fennel's unique combination of active compounds makes it an effective natural remedy for easing indigestion symptoms.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel capsules help to relieve gas and bloating in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as these symptoms can cause discomfort and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Antispasmodic: Fennel can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which can alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as cramping and spasms. The active constituents responsible for this property are alkaloids such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as inflammation can exacerbate symptoms and lead to further discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils and flavonoids.

Choleretic: Fennel can help to stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which is important for indigestion as bile helps to break down fats in the digestive tract. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone.

Diuretic: Fennel has diuretic properties that can help to eliminate excess fluid from the body, which is important for indigestion as it can help to reduce bloating and water retention in the digestive tract. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried fennel seeds and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure 0.5-1 teaspoon of the fennel powder and mix it with 1-2 teaspoons of a binding agent, such as tapioca starch or gelatin, to help the powder hold its shape.

Using a capsule filling machine or by hand, fill size "00" empty gelatin capsules with the fennel-binding agent mixture, making sure to evenly distribute the powder between the two halves of each capsule.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place before storing them in an airtight container.
Take 1-2 capsules with water after meals, as needed, for relief of indigestion symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsule helps with indigestion because it contains rosmarinic acid, which is known for its calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract. It helps to reduce bloating, gas, and discomfort by promoting the production of bile in the liver, which aids in the digestion of fats. Additionally, lemon balm has antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles of the digestive system, reducing cramping and pain. Its soothing properties also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can often exacerbate symptoms of indigestion. Overall, lemon balm capsules offer a natural and effective way to alleviate the symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lemon balm has the ability to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and discomfort, which is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate symptoms such as cramping and bloating. This property is attributed to the presence of volatile oils like citronellal and citral.

Carminative property: Lemon balm helps reduce the formation of gas in the gastrointestinal tract, which is beneficial for indigestion as it can help prevent bloating, discomfort, and pain. The carminative effect is due to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids.

Anxiolytic property: Lemon balm has been shown to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for indigestion as stress and anxiety can exacerbate symptoms. Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound, is believed to contribute to this anxiolytic effect.
Antiemetic property: Lemon balm can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of indigestion. This property is attributed to the presence of citral and other volatile oils.

Antioxidant property: Lemon balm has antioxidant properties that can help protect the gastrointestinal tract from damage caused by free radicals. This property is important for indigestion as it can help maintain the health of the digestive system and prevent further complications. The antioxidant effect is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, caffeic acid, and other phenolic compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves, or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves, and rinse them thoroughly.
Grind the lemon balm leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500mg of the lemon balm powder and mix it with an appropriate filler material, such as powdered cellulose or rice flour, to make it easier to fill the capsule shells.
Fill size 00 gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the lemon balm-filler mixture, using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Store the completed lemon balm capsules in a cool, dry place, away from direct sunlight, for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway capsule helps with indigestion because it contains caraway oil, which has carminative properties that promote the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract, providing relief from bloating, cramping, and discomfort. Additionally, caraway oil stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, facilitating the breakdown of food in the stomach and improving overall digestion. The capsule form ensures a standardized dose, making it a convenient and effective remedy for indigestion. Furthermore, caraway has been used traditionally for centuries as a digestive aid, making it a natural and time-tested solution for digestive woes.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Caraway contains volatile oil compounds such as carvone and limonene, which help to reduce bloating and gas formation in the digestive tract, providing relief from indigestion.

Antispasmodic property: Caraway contains compounds like cumuninic acid and flavonoids, which help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing cramps and spasms, thereby alleviating indigestion symptoms.

Cholagogue property: Caraway stimulates the production of bile in the liver, which aids in fat digestion, and promotes the flow of bile from the gallbladder to the small intestine, easing indigestion.
Digestive stimulant property: Caraway contains essential oils and tannins that stimulate the secretion of digestive enzymes, enhancing the digestive process and reducing indigestion symptoms.

Antimicrobial property: Caraway has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of compounds like carvacrol and thymol, which help to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut, preventing digestive issues and further supporting indigestion relief.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of caraway seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground caraway seeds with 1 cup of empty vegetarian capsule bodies.
Fill each capsule with the caraway seed powder using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool, dry place for up to 6 months.
Take 1-2 capsules with a glass of water 30 minutes before meals, as needed for indigestion relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint capsule helps with indigestion because it contains carvone and limonene, which are known to promote the production of digestive enzymes and bile, thereby facilitating the breakdown of food in the stomach. Moreover, spearmint has antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing discomfort and pain associated with indigestion. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help soothe an irritated digestive tract and protect it from damage. Overall, spearmint capsules can provide natural relief for indigestion symptoms, making it an effective and safe herbal remedy.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Spearmint capsules can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause indigestion. This is due to the presence of menthol and menthone, which have antispasmodic effects.

Carminative property: Spearmint capsules can help expel gas from the digestive tract, alleviating bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion. This is attributed to the essential oils, particularly carvone and limonene, which have carminative properties.

Choleretic property: Spearmint capsules can stimulate the production of bile in the liver, improving fat digestion and reducing the risk of indigestion. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid, a compound that increases bile secretion.

Antioxidant property: Spearmint capsules can help protect the digestive system from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the risk of indigestion. This is attributed to the high levels of rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds found in spearmint.

Anxiolytic property: Spearmint capsules can help reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to indigestion. This is due to the presence of menthol and other volatile oils, which have anxiolytic effects and can help promote relaxation and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried spearmint leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 0.5 to 1 teaspoon of the spearmint powder and place it into an empty gelatin capsule using a capsule-filling machine or by hand.
Repeat step 2 to fill additional capsules, making sure to tightly pack each one for optimal dosage.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dry place, such as a medicine cabinet or airtight container, to maintain freshness and potency.
Take 1-2 capsules as needed, typically 30 minutes before meals or as directed by a healthcare professional, to alleviate symptoms of indigestion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile capsule helps with indigestion because it contains active compounds that can help soothe the gastrointestinal tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive discomfort. These compounds include volatile oils, flavonoids, and other antioxidants that have been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines. By promoting the production of digestive enzymes and reducing muscle spasms in the gut, german chamomile capsules can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, gas, and stomach cramps. Additionally, german chamomile has been shown to have antibacterial and antiviral properties, which may help protect the digestive tract from infections that can contribute to indigestion. Overall, german chamomile capsules can provide a natural and effective way to support healthy digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile capsules can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as it can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, bloating, and discomfort. This property is due to the active constituents chamazulene and bisabolol.

Spasmolytic: German chamomile has the ability to relax smooth muscles, which can help relieve spasms in the digestive tract, thus reducing cramping and pain associated with indigestion. This property is attributed to the active constituent apigenin.

Carminative: German chamomile can help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the essential oils present in the plant, such as alpha-bisabolol and azulene.

Antioxidant: German chamomile has antioxidant properties that can help protect the digestive tract from damage, which is important for maintaining overall digestive health and alleviating indigestion symptoms. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the flavonoids and terpenoids found in the plant.

Antimicrobial: German chamomile has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, which can contribute to indigestion. This property is due to the active constituents such as chamazulene, bisabolol, and matricin.
remedy_recipe: Purchase dried German chamomile flowers and size “00” empty vegetarian capsules.
Measure 250-300mg of dried German chamomile flowers for each capsule, ensuring a consistent dosage.
Grind the measured chamomile flowers into a fine powder using a coffee or spice grinder.
Fill each capsule with the ground chamomile powder, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Store the filled capsules in a cool, dark place in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elecampane capsule helps with indigestion because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort. Additionally, elecampane stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which helps break down food more efficiently, preventing indigestion and promoting healthy digestion. Furthermore, elecampane's antibacterial properties help eliminate harmful bacteria in the gut, preventing infections and maintaining a healthy gut flora, which is essential for proper digestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Elecampane capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause indigestion. This is due to the presence of alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Carminative property: Elecampane capsules can help expel gas from the digestive tract, reducing bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion. This is attributed to the volatile oils found in elecampane.

Cholagogue property: Elecampane capsules can stimulate the production and flow of bile from the liver, aiding in the digestion of fats and reducing indigestion symptoms. This is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Antibacterial property: Elecampane capsules can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the digestive tract, reducing the risk of infections that can cause indigestion. This is attributed to the presence of alantolactone and isoalantolactone.

Anti-inflammatory property: Elecampane capsules can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, reducing pain and discomfort associated with indigestion. This is due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, such as alantolactone and isoalantolactone.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried elecampane root and grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Measure out 0.5-1 teaspoon of the elecampane powder and mix it with an equal amount of a binding agent, such as powdered psyllium husk or gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsule sizes 00 or 0 with the elecampane-binding agent mixture, using a capsule filling machine or by hand.
Allow the filled capsules to dry for 24 hours in a cool, dark place.
Store the finished elecampane capsules in an airtight container, away from direct sunlight, and take 1-2 capsules up to three times daily, as needed for indigestion relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsule helps with indigestion because it promotes the production of digestive enzymes and bile, which are essential for breaking down food in the stomach and intestines. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties can also help soothe irritation in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing discomfort and promoting healthy digestion. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to have carminative effects, which can help relieve bloating, gas, and other symptoms associated with indigestion. By supporting the digestive process and reducing inflammation, yarrow capsules can provide effective relief for individuals experiencing indigestion.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Yarrow capsules can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause indigestion, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like chamazulene and flavonoids.

Carminative property: Yarrow capsules aid in expelling gas from the stomach and intestines, alleviating bloating and discomfort often associated with indigestion, due to the active constituents like lactones and essential oils.

Astringent property: Yarrow capsules can help decrease inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, promoting healing and reducing symptoms of indigestion, attributed to tannins and sesquiterpene lactones.
Choleretic property: Yarrow capsules stimulate the production of bile, which helps in breaking down fats and preventing indigestion, owing to the presence of lactones and flavonoids.

Anxiolytic property: Yarrow capsules can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which may indirectly improve digestion by decreasing stress-induced indigestion, due to active constituents like lactones, flavonoids, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are organically grown and free of pesticides.
Grind the yarrow herb into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5-10 minutes.
Measure out 0.5 teaspoon of the ground yarrow powder and mix it with an appropriate filler material, such as tapioca starch or rice flour.
Fill size 00 empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the yarrow-filler mixture using a capsule filling machine or by hand, ensuring each capsule contains an equal amount.
Seal the capsules and store in a cool, dark place for up to six months, taking 1-2 capsules as needed, 3 times daily, before meals.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew capsule helps with indigestion because it contains parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. These properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of indigestion such as bloating, cramping, and discomfort. Additionally, feverfew has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can help improve the breakdown and absorption of food in the stomach, further easing indigestion. Furthermore, feverfew may help relax the smooth muscles of the intestines, reducing spasms and promoting regular bowel movements.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Feverfew capsules can reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate symptoms such as pain, bloating, and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property are parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antispasmodic: Feverfew can help relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate cramping, spasms, and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are parthenolide and flavonoids.

Carminative: Feverfew has the ability to reduce gas and bloating, which is important for indigestion as it can alleviate discomfort and promote the overall digestive process. The active constituents responsible for this property are volatile oils, such as camphor and camphene.

Choleretic: Feverfew can stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which is important for indigestion as it can help break down fats and improve the overall digestive process. The active constituents responsible for this property are sesquiterpene lactones, including parthenolide.

Antiemetic: Feverfew can help alleviate nausea and vomiting, which is important for indigestion as it can improve comfort and promote continued eating. The active constituents responsible for this property are parthenolide and sesquiterpene lactones.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 fresh feverfew leaves and let them wilt for a day to reduce their bitterness.
Grind the wilted leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Measure out 500mg of the feverfew powder and encapsulate it in a size "00" gelatin capsule.
Repeat steps 2-3 for a total of 12 capsules, resulting in a batch of feverfew capsules.
Store the capsules in a cool, dark place, and take 1-2 capsules as needed for indigestion.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat indigestion with herbal capsules is by using those made from ginger, peppermint, or chamomile. These natural remedies have been shown to reduce inflammation, relax gastrointestinal muscles, and relieve symptoms of indigestion. Always follow the recommended dosage on the product label and consult with a healthcare provider if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking other medications.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to indigestion that are treated with herbal capsules include acid reflux, bloating, and gastritis. Herbal capsules often contain natural ingredients such as ginger, fennel, and peppermint, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. For example, ginger has been shown to reduce inflammation and nausea, while fennel can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce gas. Peppermint oil, on the other hand, has been found to improve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, which can include bloating, abdominal pain, and changes in bowel movements. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before taking any herbal supplements to ensure safety and effectiveness.
